_comment: Regression fixture for the LendingClub Locke demo. Each entry names an E-code that MUST appear in the Locke report when run against the full LendingClub 2007-2018 accepted-loans CSV after `binarize_loan_status` filters to terminal-outcome rows. The integration test in tests/expected_findings.rs verifies (a) every required code fires at least min_count times, (b) every code in fingerprint_columns fires against the listed column(s). Empirically calibrated against report.json from a full-dataset run on 2026-06-01 (~2.26M rows loaded, 1.33M terminal-outcome rows kept). See cross_validate.md for the measurement provenance.

required_findings.description: Auto-detected string sentinels — `"n/a"` on `emp_length`, empty-string on the joint-application columns, and similar across ~55 Str columns. NOTE: the handoff predicted E9008 would fire on `term` due to its leading-space `" 36 months"` artifact, but E9008 detects missing-value SENTINELS specifically (`?`/`NA`/etc.), not leading-whitespace formatting issues, so `term` is not in the fingerprint set.

High missingness — co-borrower columns (annual_inc_joint, dti_joint, verification_status_joint, etc.) are empty for the ~85% of loans without a co-applicant; hardship/settlement columns are also sparse.

Suspicious target leakage (0.85 ≤ |AUC| < 0.95). The top signals are the LAST REFRESHED FICO range (`last_fico_range_high`/`low`) — these reflect the borrower's credit AFTER the loan, so a charged-off loan's borrower typically shows a depressed FICO. `total_rec_prncp` is the principal recovered to date — high for paid, low for defaulted. The handoff predicted `total_pymnt` and other payment streams would dominate at AUC ≥ 0.95; in this dataset they instead land below the 0.85 threshold because their value is also dominated by loan size and term duration, not just outcome.

ID-like cardinality — distinct/n_rows ≥ 0.95. `id` is the loan key. `url` carries the per-loan listing URL with the loan ID embedded, so it ALSO has near-perfect cardinality — a separate-but-related leakage hint Locke surfaces independently. `member_id` does NOT fire because the same member can hold multiple loans.

v0.8 (ADR-0042) auto-promotion: Str columns whose non-sentinel values are mostly parseable as f64 are converted to Float-with-NaN. On LC, this fires on 22 columns including the secondary-applicant set (sec_app_*), the hardship workout fields (hardship_*), settlement_*, revol_bal_joint, mths_since_last_record, and orig_projected_additional_accrued_interest. IMPORTANT: it does NOT fire on annual_inc_joint / dti_joint / verification_status_joint despite their being numeric in spirit. The CSV reader does not handle quoted strings, so commas inside the `desc` (free-text loan description) column shift subsequent columns left, polluting later columns with `application_type` values like 'Individual'. The parseable-fraction check correctly refuses to promote in that case. The right upstream fix is RFC-4180 quoting support in `cjc-data::CsvReader`; out of scope for ADR-0042.

v0.8 (ADR-0042 part 2) wires `detect_conditional_missingness` into the pipeline (it was defined but never called pre-v0.8). After auto-promotion + the wiring fix, E9070 fires across the secondary-applicant column set and other co-missing Float pairs (sec_app_*, hardship_*, settlement_*). The pairwise count is large (~1800 firings, ~54 unique columns) because there are ~30 Float columns whose NaN positions perfectly align — every ordered pair fires. Consumers who want a smaller set can raise `ConditionalMissingnessConfig.implication_threshold` above 0.95 or filter to specific column pairs in post-processing.

_notes: E9062 ('target not binary') must NOT fire — we binarize loan_status into target_default upstream.

E9060 (|AUC| ≥ 0.95) does NOT fire in this dataset. The handoff predicted it would on `total_pymnt`/`recoveries`/etc., but the empirical AUCs all land in [0.85, 0.95] (warning band) at most. This is a real finding: the post-origination payment streams are leaky but not deterministically so, because loan size + term duration spreads their distribution.

E9070 (conditional missingness) NOW FIRES on the *secondary-applicant* columns as of v0.8 (ADR-0042). The earlier note that it would NOT fire was correct for v0.7 and earlier — the gap was three-fold: (a) the CSV reader typed numeric-but-empty-first-row cols as Str, blinding the Float-NaN check, (b) `detect_conditional_missingness` was defined but never called from validate_dataframe, and (c) the CSV reader's lack of quoted-string support means columns sitting after the `desc` (free-text) field get polluted, so promotion still skips them. ADR-0042 ships fixes for (a) and (b); fix (c) is upstream in cjc-data::CsvReader and out of scope.

E9009 (v0.8) is the auto-promotion notice. It MUST fire on ≥10 columns. The actual fingerprint set is the secondary-applicant fields (sec_app_*) and hardship/settlement workout fields, NOT the joint-application columns. If E9009 fires zero times, ValidationConfig::auto_promote_str_to_float was disabled OR the CSV columns no longer match the sentinel-rich-first-row pattern.
fingerprint_columns is the canonical column the E-code MUST fire on. The fixture treats extra firings on other columns as acceptable noise (LC has ~150 cols and many co-vary).
min_count is a lower bound. Locke is allowed to fire more findings per code than listed; the gate fails only on UNDER-firing.
If E9060 starts firing on any column in a future run, that's a story worth investigating — the leakage signal will have crossed from suspicious to near-deterministic.
